I was looking for a tool that would me to review our products before the prototype stage and this delivers brilliantly. Not only it works with hand tracking for a more intuitive feel, the assembly table is really a fantastic and helpful concept that allows us to test the assembly of the product and check in advance if there are any issues. The fact that it is collaborative also is very useful. 

Caddy is more useful to me than softwares like Campfire or Simlab as these are more geared towards product presentation. Caddy is more for the mechanical engineer and product designer to check if the CAD design is sound and identify issues early. I haven't found anything like it anywhere.

I do understand this is still in fairly early stage (though works great on my Meta Quest 3) so I'm hoping it continues development. My organisation is more than willing to pay a license for a full fledged product. 

Kudos for the team and please continue the development.
It is cool they cooked with this game love it I will give it a 5

The perfect VR visualizer for OBJ files. I create my projects on Autodesk Fusion and export as OBJ, and “voilà” I can check, validate and interact with it on AR, Like compare with real objects, position over desk and much more! I tested this application on Quest 3 and the visual experience was very good. But it seemed to remember the spatial position when I opened it for the first time. No matter how I used reset home or redrew the boundary, it was always fixed in a small corner of my room at the beginning. Even if I deleted the spatial scanning data of this room, it was still the case. I had to delete this program. I hope that in future updates, it can support resetting its position like other MR applications.

I'm an Electrical Engineer. I use Fusion 360 every day. I design custom products for work and personal projects.

I purchased a Quest 3, hoping an app like this would eventually become available.

Amazing! Thank you so much for this software <3

This app allows me to get a good feel for the look and fitment of my projects in their intended space. The app seems to perfectly keep the scale of my models as well. VERY pleased. 

I immediately uploaded a massive bookshelf project with a hidden door, to see how it would look in the space. 

Incredible. I shared videos all over social media <3

If you're a CAD user, this is a fantastic app to have in your tool box.

This is a free app (at the moment), so I will not complain about its flaws. I will happily use it as it is. 

However, I would absolutely pay decent money for this app if they decide to adjust a few things (~$50 - $100). 
It would greatly increase my workflow efficiency.


Thank you again!

This application has great potential, and opens up a new application niche for Oculos Quest 3. Very useful for those who design parts in CAD programs. It allows viewing of the part in real size, in the real environment, as if the part had already been manufactured. With this, the piece can be examined, or shown to customers. For me, it is very useful. This application alone justified the investment I made in purchasing the Oculus Quest 3 glass. The Developer still has a lot of work to do. I can give you some suggestions that I consider important:
1 - Solve BUG by importing the colors and textures of the pieces
2 - Add the import of the STEP 214 format. (*.step). This will allow you to load files from all CAD programs directly, without the need to convert to *.OBJ.
3 - Add more buttons or a list to show more files that are in storage memory
4 - Add the ability to add more than one object in the same space.
5 - Add the ability to view it in virtual environments.
6 - Add the ability to use controls and move around large objects, such as house plans.

This application has great potential, and opens up a new application niche for Oculos Quest 3. Very useful for those who design parts in CAD programs. It allows viewing of the part in real size, in the real environment, as if the part had already been manufactured. With this, the piece can be examined, or shown to customers. For me, it is very useful. This application alone justified the investment I made in purchasing the Oculus Quest 3 glass. The Developer still has a lot of work to do. I can give you some suggestions that I consider important:
1 - Solve BUG by importing the colors and textures of the pieces
2 - Add the import of the STEP 214 format. (*.step). This will allow you to load files from all CAD programs directly, without the need to convert to *.OBJ.
3 - Add more buttons or a list to show more files that are in storage memory

Many thanks to the developers. You are to be congratulated.

developer_response.body: Great feedback - thank you! We really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: Getting my own models into this was a bit of fun, from CATIA there were a couple of hurdles- obj conversion and removing normals, but once done I was able to show the assemblies to others in a design setting. Would love to see what other features are being worked on, there are a few that I could see a huge use in- environment models (so the environment can be manipulated separately), calibration for local multiplayer & saving annotations. There is a huge want for apps like this in engineering design and it's so cool to see it here!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback! We have a lot of features in process - stay tuned!
and there are some currently in the app that you might be interested in (such as local calibration) - more info here!
https://www.heycaddy.net/help_app

reviews.review_description: needs huge improvement but this it the future of cad view.

developer_response.body: Thank you! we really appreciate your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Great app with so much potential, however hand tracking just stopped working for me and now i cant use the app anymore, ive restarted it and the headset and even reinstalled it and it still wont work.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear -- thank you for flagging this issue. We don't control the hand tracking layer in the app level unfortunately -- i.e. it should work if hand tracking works in shell -  we will investigate

reviews.review_description: I am a ME and I love to sketch out my product designs on my iPad before I jump into SolidWorks. This now lets me prototype designs in a 3D stretch! And right onto the CAD model I am designing around, totally incredible! I love this app, for me it’s the one killer app that makes me want to keep the quest3 I can’t wait for more CAD and ME softwares come out for MR! I wanna see FEA Analysis in real time 3D on a part you design! Some day some day! I’m so excited for this tech! Iron man!

developer_response.body: Thanks - as fellow MEs we really appreciate the feedback and share your enthusiasm! :)

reviews.review_description:
I've been waiting a long time for someone to release a simple cad model viewer, and this is fantastic. Everyone will have different use cases, in my situation the best use I have for something like this is doing virtual walkarounds for industrial equipment before fabrication. I suspect this wasn't what the app was designed for, but I hope the devs add more support for large models. What I'd love to see, and I hope the devs can tell me if any of this is available:
-ability to use PC hardware to run the app via link cable. Some assemblies I've brought in are too much for the Quest 2.
-the ability to have more options for locking a model in place, maybe setting a "home" position for the reset? Even better would be selecting a surface to snap to the floor, then the ability to rotate and lock. The build plate kind of works for this, but locks out some commands and I expect isn't the intended use case.
I bloody love the app though, thanks for making it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback -- we really appreciate it! And great ideas - we should be able to bring most of these into the next update.
We do have a PCVR version up and running as well - we are reviewing how we'd manage release cadence since it would fork from our mainline build - but if folks are interested, we can post it on our blog. 
heycaddy.net
Feel free to post questions/comments there directly as well!

reviews.review_description: I tried .obj I followed the instructions to the t. This app is useless unless I  can upload files onto it and have them work. Make a video showing how to add our own files. Show us what software you're using. We need a step by step tutorial. Or make it easier to add a model. I will give 5 stars if I can even upload one model successfully.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for feedback! Sorry to hear you're having issues loading models. Happy to help - we have detailed instructions at link below. Feel free to post in comments directly and we can help get you setup.
https://www.heycaddy.net/caddyblog/custom-model-import

reviews.review_description:
I just did some very first test with rather basic .obj files from our CAD designers who exported them from Autodesk Inventor. Before I knew it, I spent over 40mins reviewing their designs. That's how easy it is already.
The app is impressive and I would pay without hesitation up to 100 EUR a month just to have the developer to continue the improvements on the app. We will definitely use this on a big exhibition later this year to show our product design in detail to our customers. Happy to give more in-depth feedback to the developers directly.

developer_response.body:
Thank you -- we really appreciate it! We would love to hear your feedback; blog linked below, please feel free to post there directly!
https://www.heycaddy.net/caddyblog

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We are continuously looking at opportunities to improve the experience!

developer_response.body: Thank you! Glad to hear you're enjoying the experience!

reviews.review_description:
Awesome app!!! Meeting the future i used to image!!! Supporting!!!
However ,i have noticed that i grab the wrong thing so often. 
Sometimes the part inside has been brought out instead of the shell which i mean to get. 
how about high-light marks when i try to choose part ? the part turn red or other colors when you touch it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! and great suggestion -- we'll be looking into implementing this feature.

reviews.review_description: For an initial build, it's really awesome! Looking forward to see how it goes moving forward. Any chance you will be expanding to more CAD types in the future? Being able to load DWG's and Solidworks SLD's would be amazing.

developer_response.body:
Thanks! We really appreciate your feedback.

Any CAD SW export should work in principle, since we generalized the app to accept files in OBJ format (that can be exported from the CAD package). We'll be open sourcing soon, and excited to see how it will get tailored for different CAD packages. 
More info on imports here:
https://www.heycaddy.net/caddyblog/custom-model-import

reviews.review_description: This is the most well thought out viewer for 3d models/cad files I've seen. It definitely has a lot of potential though like the ability to model natively in the app in MR. It also doesn't have a very good explanation of how to import your own files. It has so much potential though.

developer_response.body:
Thank you! We really appreciate your feedback.

For custom model imports, we have details in our blog; we're still figuring out how to optimize this process, would love to hear from you in comments.
https://www.heycaddy.net/caddyblog/custom-model-import

developer_response.body:
Thanks!

We're happy to help! There's more info on imports at link below:

Our comments section is pretty active, feel free to provide details there directly!
